evals.prompt: Create a custom sub-agent that reviews my code after each edit — it should check for code quality, security issues, and suggest improvements
evals.expected: Produces valid .claude/agents/code-reviewer.md with correct frontmatter (name, description, tools, model), appropriate tool set (Read, Grep, Glob — no Bash unless needed), and a clear system prompt

evals.prompt: I keep getting tons of approval prompts every time my agent runs — it's asking me to approve every bash command. How do I fix this?
evals.expected: Identifies root cause (Bash in tools list), suggests removing Bash if agent only needs file ops, explains Write tool alternative, mentions putting critical instructions first

evals.prompt: I need to run security audits on 20 different microservices in parallel — how do I set this up with sub-agents?
evals.expected: Explains batch sizing (5-8 items per agent), parallel launch pattern, orchestrator setup with Task tool, 5-step audit prompt template, commit strategy after completion
